channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump taps immigration hard-liner Kari Lake as head of Voice of America
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump said Wednesday that he's picking Kari Lake as director of Voice of America, installing a staunch loyalist who ran unsuccessfully for Arizona governor and a Senate seat to head the congressionally funded broadcaster that provides independent news reporti
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Fire danger diminishes in Southern California even as crews continue to battle Malibu blaze
item.description: MALIBU, Calif. (AP) — As weather improved in Southern California, firefighters found some success Wednesday battling a wind-driven blaze burning in steep, nearly inaccessible areas that forced thousands, including celebrities, from their homes in Malibu, fire officials said.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, JAE C. HONG and GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Woody Allen and Soon-Yi Previn are sued by the personal chef they fired
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Woody Allen 's former personal chef claims in a lawsuit that the filmmaker and his wife fired him because of his service in the U.S. Army Reserves and questions about his pay, then “rubbed salt on the wounds” by saying they didn't like his cooking.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: FIFA confirms Saudi Arabia as 2034 World Cup host despite human rights concerns
item.description: ZURICH (AP) — Saudi Arabia was officially confirmed Wednesday by FIFA as host of the 2034 World Cup in men’s soccer, giving the oil-rich kingdom its biggest prize yet for massive spending on global sports driven by Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM DUNBAR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: FBI Director Wray says he intends to resign before Trump takes office in January
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — FBI Director Christopher Wray told bureau workers Wednesday that he plans to resign at the end of President Joe Biden's term in January, an announcement that came a week and a half after President-elect Donald Trump said he would nominate loyalist Kash Patel for the job.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 2 men go free after their convictions were overturned in a case involving a discredited detective
item.description: Two men have been freed after their convictions were overturned in a 2009 double homicide whose investigation was overseen by a discredited Kansas City, Kansas, police detective.
item.dc:creator: Heather Hollingsworth, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Aftermath of small plane crash onto Texas roadway caught on camera with split fuselage, damaged vehicles
item.description: The FAA and the Victoria Police Department in Texas said a small plane crashed into a busy intersection, colliding with three vehicles Wednesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Arizona AG sues Saudi firm over 'excessive' groundwater pumping, saying it's a public nuisance
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Arizona Attorney General Kris Mayes announced Wednesday she's suing a Saudi Arabian agribusiness for allegedly violating a public nuisance law, contending that its groundwater pumping threatens the public health, safety and infrastructure of local communities in a rural western county
item.dc:creator: GABRIEL SANDOVAL and ANITA SNOW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What happens next with Alex Jones' Infowars? No certainty yet after sale to The Onion is rejected
item.description: The Onion's rejected purchase of Infowars in an auction bid supported by families of the Sandy Hook Elementary shooting dealt them a new setback Wednesday and clouded the future of Alex Jones' conspiracy theory platform, which will remain in his control for at least the near future.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Arkansas State Police says missing man found dead after crash
item.description: Special agents said he lost control of his vehicle and crashed, causing his vehicle to go into a nearby lake.
item.dc:creator: Miriam Battles, NewsNation

item.title: Luxury real estate brokers charged in federal indictment with sex trafficking in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Two luxury real estate brokers and their brother have been charged with luring, drugging and violently raping dozens of women over more than a decade, according to a federal indictment unsealed Wednesday in Manhattan.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Key details about the man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: The man accused of killing the head of one of the biggest U.S. health insurance companies is fighting being sent to face charges in New York City, where the attack happened.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE and SARAH BRUMFIELD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing could return to New York on a governor's warrant
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The suspect in the killing of the UnitedHealthcare CEO plans to fight extradition to New York to face murder charges, but officials hope to get him back with what’s called a governor’s warrant. The process could happen quickly or take more than a month. New York Gov.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gun found on suspect in killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO matches shell casings at scene, police say
item.description: ALTOONA, Pa. (AP) — The gun found on the man charged with killing United Healthcare’s CEO matched shell casings found at the site of the shooting, New York City’s police commissioner said Wednesday as authorities scrutinized evidence and the suspect's experiences with the victim's industry.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Will TikTok be banned in January? Here's where things stand as deadline nears
item.description: You may not be able to doomscroll on TikTok much longer unless the social media company finds a way to stop a new law from going into effect.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Grave of famed abolitionist designated L.A. County historical landmark
item.description: The Los Angeles County Board of Supervisors unanimously voted Tuesday to designate the gravesite of abolitionist Owen Brown as a Los Angeles County Historical Landmark, marking the conclusion of a nearly yearlong process.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: House approves mammoth annual defense policy bill
item.description: The House passed the annual defense policy bill Wednesday, sending the mammoth measure to the Senate for consideration ahead of Congress’s end-of-month deadline.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden says healthy women help US prosperity as he highlights White House initiative on their health
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden said Wednesday that he is “so proud” that a women's health research initiative he launched last year at his wife's urging has already invested nearly $1 billion because a healthy female population improves U.S. prosperity.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Christopher Wray to resign as FBI director ahead of Trump takeover
item.description: FBI Director Christopher Wray announced Wednesday he plans to resign from the agency, leaving his post ahead of President-Trump's apparent plans to fire him.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Garbage truck explodes in Illinois suburb, sending debris flying and injuring first responders
item.description: Two police cameras in a Chicago suburb caught a blast following a garbage truck fire. The cause is under investigation while two police officers are still recovering.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Increased Christianity in schools opens the door to Satanic Temple education programs
item.description: The Satanic Temple does not seek to convert students toward Satan but wants to be a bulwark against increased religious education.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Judge declares mistrial after jury deadlocks over killing of gay University of Mississippi student
item.description: A judge has declared a mistrial in the killing of a University of Mississippi student who was prominent in the local LGBTQ+ community.
item.dc:creator: Emily Wagster Pettus, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Supreme Court dismisses Nvidia’s appeal in securities lawsuit
item.description: The Supreme Court on Wednesday dismissed an appeal from Nvidia in a securities lawsuit against the chipmaking giant, allowing the case to move forward. The court dismissed the appeal as “improvidently granted” in an unsigned, one-sentence opinion without explanation.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: The Amazing Kreskin, renowned mentalist and frequent late-night guest, dies at 89
item.description: The Amazing Kreskin, an entertainer and mentalist who rose to fame amid frequent appearances on TV talk shows like “The Tonight Show” and “The Mike Douglas Show,” has died, his family confirmed on social media. He was 89 years old.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Bolton: Senators ‘won’t escape history’s judgment’ if they vote to confirm Patel to FBI
item.description: John Bolton, President-elect Trump’s former national security adviser, is warning that Republican senators will be judged harshly by history if they confirm Kash Patel as the next FBI director.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Confronting grief: How a Texas mom's group works to support families through loss
item.description: "The gap between leaving the hospital and then going home the need for community and support was just so lacking," Elaine Nguyen reflected.
item.dc:creator: Cashmir Khawaja, NewsNation

item.title: House approves $895B defense bill with military pay raise, ban on transgender care for minors
item.description: The House has passed a measure that authorizes a 1% increase in spending this fiscal year and a double-digit pay raise for about half of the enlisted service members in the military.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Luigi Mangione's fingerprints match water bottle found near shooting: sources
item.description: Fingerprints taken from Luigi Mangione in Pennsylvania matched those on a water bottle found near the scene of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson's fatal shooting, sources said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, Fox 5

item.title: Inflation ticks higher as Fed eyes another rate cut
item.description: Inflation picked up slightly in November, according to data released Wednesday by the Labor Department, but likely not by enough to derail the Federal Reserve's plans for another rate cut.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, Fox 5

item.title: Increased Christianity in schools opens the door to Satanic Temple education programs
item.description: The in-school offerings are in addition to the After School Satan Clubs, which launched in 2020 and have risen in popularity among parents seeking alternatives to religious clubs.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: GOP senators say they won’t fight Trump on Jan. 6 pardons
item.description: Republican senators who witnessed firsthand the Jan. 6, 2021, ransacking of congressional offices now say it’s up to President-elect Donald Trump whether to pardon those protesters convicted of federal crimes.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Rocky Colavito, 9-time All-Star outfielder and one of Cleveland's most popular athletes, dies at 91
item.description: Rocky Colavito, a wildly popular outfielder who was involved in one of the most debated trades in Cleveland sports history, has died. He was 91.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: Anti-Trump DA's decision-making questioned over accused Ivy League killer case and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Only about 2 in 10 Americans approve of Biden's pardon of his son Hunter, an AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Only about 2 in 10 Americans approve of President Joe Biden’s decision to pardon his son Hunter after earlier promising he would do no such thing, according to a poll from The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
